method: cluster by creative-unit → gates (recurrence≥2 creators, transfer≥2 niches, performance≥corpus-median vpf) → agent-named formula+3-part name

trends.name: Silent caption-card relatable confession scroll-stopper
trends.formula: Cold-open on a charged scene → big white text states the feeling, not the product → no voiceover so the viewer reads → caption finishes the joke/relate → implicit save-this payoff
trends.whyItTravels: Text carries the message so any niche can swap the scene while keeping the same read-and-relate mechanic that works with sound off.

trends.name: Screen-recording insider-tool curiosity walkthrough
trends.formula: Open on a screen/app/desk-setup mid-action → text offers a specific thing most people don't know → record the steps as proof → withhold the best part → reveal payoff on screen
trends.whyItTravels: An over-the-shoulder screen makes a claim feel like leaked insider knowledge regardless of whether it's software or styling.

trends.name: Handheld POV myth-bust that dares you to disagree
trends.formula: Phone-in-hand close talk → state a belief everyone holds → 'actually you're wrong' turn → walk through the contrarian evidence → land the corrected belief
trends.whyItTravels: Challenging a shared assumption provokes comment-section debate, and the casual handheld framing reads as honest across food, skin, hair, home and fitness.

trends.name: Handheld first-person before/after reveal swing
trends.formula: Show the ugly/messy 'before' up close → narrate the frustration in first person → apply the fix on camera → swing the phone to the clean 'after' → let the contrast be the payoff
trends.whyItTravels: Watching a state flip from bad to good is a universal satisfaction the viewer can verify with their own eyes, no niche knowledge required.

trends.name: Caption-card transformation reveal with no narration
trends.formula: Static 'before' frame → big text labels the problem → hard cut to the 'after' → text labels the result → caption seals the promise, audio optional
trends.whyItTravels: The widest cross-niche cluster: text-titled before/after needs no language or voice, so the same skeleton ships across pets, food, home, fashion, fitness, tech and hair.

trends.name: Named-pain caption-card rescue before/after
trends.formula: Name a specific painful problem in text → agitate it with the worst-case 'before' → introduce the fix → show the wipe/swap/result → text confirms the relief
trends.whyItTravels: Explicitly naming the pain pre-qualifies the viewer, then the visible fix proves it, which transfers cleanly across home, hair, pets and fitness.

trends.name: Caption-card lifestyle moment with no explicit hook
trends.formula: Drop into an everyday moment → text labels what we're seeing → let the scene play → minor turn or detail → caption closes the loop
trends.whyItTravels: A low-effort text label over ordinary footage reads as authentic 'just my life' content that the feed rewards across gym, home, bedroom and pet settings.

trends.name: Hold-it-up product demo with a direct you-callout
trends.formula: Point the product at camera → call out 'you' / the exact buyer → demo the one thing it does → show it working → 'links below' nudge
trends.whyItTravels: The largest creator cluster: a direct callout + a clean single-feature demo is the default TikTok-Shop selling unit that works in any product category.

trends.name: Lifestyle b-roll this-vs-that comparison montage
trends.formula: Aesthetic b-roll establishes the scene → set up two options side by side → show the contrast in use → tip the verdict to the winner → soft payoff
trends.whyItTravels: Comparison gives the viewer a verdict to agree or argue with, and pretty lifestyle b-roll keeps it watchable across pets and fashion.

trends.name: Aspirational b-roll that removes the usual barrier
trends.formula: Aspirational lifestyle b-roll → name the limitation people assume ('you can't / you need X') → reveal the thing that removes it → show the now-possible result → invite belonging
trends.whyItTravels: Selling 'you no longer need the hard/expensive prerequisite' is a universal unlock framed over desirable b-roll, transferring across fitness, supplements, pets and fashion.

trends.name: Product demo that erases an everyday limitation
trends.formula: Open on the daily annoyance → demo the product removing it ('without having to...') → show the freed-up result → repeat the use cases → 'just try it'
trends.whyItTravels: Pairing a concrete limitation-removal with a literal on-camera demo gives both promise and proof in one beat, working from beauty to home to tech.

trends.name: Voiceover-over-b-roll did-you-know value drop
trends.formula: Calm voiceover starts a fact → tactile/aesthetic b-roll holds the eye → 'here's what nobody tells you' value → stack 1-2 more points → soft takeaway to save
trends.whyItTravels: Voiceover carries an information promise while ritual b-roll holds retention, a combo that saves well across fitness, supplements, food, pets, beauty and home.

trends.name: Talking-head named-buyer pain agitation to fix
trends.formula: Face to camera → call out the exact person ('if you have X') → agitate how bad the problem is → present the fix → tell them what to do next
trends.whyItTravels: Direct talking-head callout plus problem-agitation is the highest-conviction persuasion unit for body/appearance niches like beauty, skin, supplements and hair.

trends.name: Lifestyle b-roll industry-secret contrarian reframe
trends.formula: Aesthetic b-roll → 'the industry doesn't want you to know' contrarian claim → reframe the conventional wisdom → give the alternative → leave them feeling let-in
trends.whyItTravels: Anti-establishment 'they're lying to you' framing earns trust and shares, and over neutral b-roll it ports across supplements, fashion, fitness, pets and food.

trends.name: Product demo backed by borrowed authority
trends.formula: Open with an authority cue (expert/doctor/'I used to work for') → demo the product → tie it to the authority's reasoning → show it in use → recommend with credibility
trends.whyItTravels: Borrowing a credible figure lowers skepticism, and a real demo supplies the proof, a combo that transfers across beauty, home, food, supplements and skin.

trends.name: Voiceover-b-roll pain story that unlocks a fix
trends.formula: Voiceover sets up a relatable frustration → b-roll shows the messy situation → name the limitation → reveal the product removing it → quiet relief payoff
trends.whyItTravels: A spoken micro-story makes the limitation feel real before the fix lands, working anywhere the pain is concrete like home and pets.

trends.name: Get-the-result-without-the-cost demo promise
trends.formula: Name the desired result → 'without [the expensive/slow/annoying way]' → demo the shortcut product → show it delivering → close on the saved time/money
trends.whyItTravels: The X-without-Y frame is a clean value proposition (same outcome, none of the cost) that demos well across beauty, skin, home, food and tech.

trends.name: Handheld POV in-the-moment slice with no setup
trends.formula: Phone already rolling on a real moment → no hook, just drop the viewer in → let the action/reaction play → small turn or punchline → end abruptly
trends.whyItTravels: Hookless, raw POV reads as unstaged real life that the feed treats as native, transferring across gym, car, appliance and hallway moments in any niche.
